body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Re-enable folding after size threshold is crossed downward**

When the document is over `MaximumAutomaticFoldingDocumentLength`, this branch tears down folding and unsubscribes from folding-change events, but there is no subsequent path that reinstalls automatic folding when edits shrink the same document back under the limit (normal edits only hit `OnDocumentChanged`, which does not call `InstallAutomaticFolding`). In practice, a large file that gets trimmed below the threshold will keep `FoldingManager` null until callers manually toggle a folding property or replace the document.

Useful? React with ≡ƒæì┬á/ ≡ƒæÄ.

`ChangeMayAffectFoldings_Brace_Returns_True_For_Braces` currently has an unused local (`strategy`) and no assertions/coverage. With `TreatWarningsAsErrors=true`, the unused variable warning will fail the build; either complete the test (assert `strategy.ChangeMayAffectFoldings(...)` via a real `DocumentChangeEventArgs`/editor path) or remove it.

`MaximumAutomaticFoldingDocumentLength` accepts negative (or zero) values without validation, which makes automatic folding permanently disable (because `Document.TextLength > value` is always true for negatives) and is inconsistent with other Editor size properties (e.g., `IndentationSize` validates >= 1). Add an `ArgumentOutOfRangeException.ThrowIfLessThan` guard (>= 0 or >= 1, whichever is intended).

In `InstallAutomaticFolding`, the teardown path clears `FoldingManager` when `_automaticFolding` is true even if `_foldingStrategy` is null. This can unexpectedly wipe a consumer-provided `FoldingManager` if they toggle `AutomaticFolding=true` before/without setting a strategy. Consider tracking whether the current `FoldingManager` was created/owned by automatic folding (e.g., keep a private `_automaticFoldingManager` reference) and only clear it when itΓÇÖs the owned instance.

`Dispose` unsubscribes from the folding document events via `SetFoldingDocument(null)`, but it does not clear `FoldingManager`. Since `Editor` subscribes to `FoldingManager.FoldingChanged` in the property setter, a `FoldingManager` instance held externally could keep a disposed `Editor` alive. Consider setting `FoldingManager = null` during disposal to ensure the event handler is detached.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Track FoldingManager ownership on external assignment**

`InstallAutomaticFolding` marks the manager as owned (`_automaticFoldingOwnsFoldingManager = true`) whenever auto-folding installs one, but there is no path that clears ownership if a consumer later assigns `editor.FoldingManager` manually. In that scenario, a subsequent auto-folding state change (e.g., `AutomaticFolding = false`, strategy unset, or threshold exceeded) will treat the consumer-provided manager as auto-owned and clear it unexpectedly, even though the comment says externally set managers must not be wiped. This regresses manual folding-manager integration after auto-folding has been enabled once.

Useful? React with ≡ƒæì┬á/ ≡ƒæÄ.
